D6 Display L6 Create Reference

Foveated Rendering for VR

the eye only sees sharply at its centre — so render full detail at the gaze point and let the periphery go coarse, unnoticed

Human acuity falls off steeply away from the fovea. A VR headset with eye tracking exploits this: it renders the gaze point at full resolution and drops detail with eccentricity, matching the eye's own falloff. Done right, the periphery looks unchanged while the GPU draws far fewer pixels.

Move the gaze point across the scene and raise the foveation strength. Watch the periphery coarsen while the centre stays crisp, and read how much pixel work is saved. Push too hard and the blocky edge creeps into where you can notice it.
